title: WSET Knowledge Map — Causal Chain
description: A structured, multi-step cause-and-effect sequence that traces how one wine factor leads to an outcome through traceable intermediate steps. Causal chains are central to Distinction-level reasoning.

chain_id.description: Stable unique identifier. Format: CC_{SLUG} — e.g. CC_COOL_CLIMATE_ACIDITY

chain_name.description: Short human-readable label for this chain.

starting_factor.description: The initial condition or cause that drives the chain.

concept_id.description: concept_id of the starting factor concept.

description.description: How the starting factor is expressed in this chain.

intermediate_steps.description: Ordered list of intermediate mechanisms between the starting factor and the final outcome. Each step is a link in the causal chain.

concept_id.description: concept_id of the concept at this step.

description.description: What happens at this step and why.

relationship_type.description: The type of causal link from the previous step to this one.

final_outcome.description: The end result of the causal chain.

description.description: How the outcome manifests in the wine.

examples.description: Real-world wine examples that illustrate this causal chain.

related_exam_questions.description: Types or topics of WSET L3 exam questions where understanding this chain is important.

distinction_note.description: What a Distinction-level answer adds to the description of this chain that a Pass answer would omit.

complexity.description: How many inferential steps are required to trace this chain fully.

tutor_use.description: Whether the Tutor Agent should use this chain in explanations.

examiner_use.description: Whether the Examiner Agent may use this chain. False unless the chain is directly derivable from official WSET sources only.
